title: 6.2.2 Carbon Cycle
description: Carbon cycle — every fact from the Grade 10 Biology textbook.

questions.question: What gases is the air around us composed of?
options: Oxygen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, and inert gases
questions.correctAnswer: Oxygen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, and inert gases

questions.question: Which two gases are particularly important for the survival of living organisms?
What two processes maintain the amount of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the atmosphere?
How is the amount of carbon in the atmosphere maintained?
options: By processes that continuously take it from and release it back to the atmosphere
Only by processes that release it
Only by processes that absorb it
questions.correctAnswer: By processes that continuously take it from and release it back to the atmosphere

questions.question: What do green plants absorb from the air during photosynthesis?
What happens to the carbon stored in plants?
options: It is transferred to animals that eat the plant
It stays in the plant forever
questions.correctAnswer: It is transferred to animals that eat the plant

questions.question: What releases carbon dioxide back to the air when plants and animals die?
What do all living organisms, including plants and animals, release throughout their entire lifetime?
What do dead bodies of organisms become over a long period of time?
What activities release carbon dioxide by burning fossil fuels?
What is the chemical formula for carbon dioxide?
Is $CO_2$ a natural part of Earth's atmosphere?
options: Yes, it is a natural part of Earth's atmosphere
It is only found in cities
It was never present before the industrial era
questions.correctAnswer: Yes, it is a natural part of Earth's atmosphere

questions.question: Since when have atmospheric $CO_2$ concentrations significantly increased?
What are the major sources of increased $CO_2$?
options: Burning of fossil fuels (coal, oil, natural gas) and wood for energy
questions.correctAnswer: Burning of fossil fuels (coal, oil, natural gas) and wood for energy

questions.question: How does atmospheric $CO_2$ act in terms of heat?
options: It acts like a heat-trapping blanket, absorbing heat and holding it in
It has no effect on heat
questions.correctAnswer: It acts like a heat-trapping blanket, absorbing heat and holding it in

questions.question: What is global warming?
options: A cooling of the earth's surface
A rise in the overall temperature of the earth's surface
questions.correctAnswer: A rise in the overall temperature of the earth's surface

questions.question: Which gas is the most important heat-trapping (greenhouse) gas?
What are the other greenhouse gases mentioned besides $CO_2$?
What is the root cause of climate change according to the textbook?
options: Addition of excessive $CO_2$ into the atmosphere due to human activity
questions.correctAnswer: Addition of excessive $CO_2$ into the atmosphere due to human activity

questions.question: What are the two major processes that maintain the balance of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the atmosphere?
Where does photosynthesis occur in a plant cell?
options: In the chloroplast of the leaf cell
questions.correctAnswer: In the chloroplast of the leaf cell

questions.question: Where does aerobic cellular respiration take place in a cell?
What are the reactants of photosynthesis?
What are the products of photosynthesis?
What are the reactants of cellular respiration?
What are the products of cellular respiration?
What two things are needed for photosynthesis besides carbon dioxide and water?
What is the balanced chemical equation for photosynthesis?
What is the balanced chemical equation for cellular respiration?
Which human activities interfere with the carbon-oxygen cycle according to the textbook?
options: Illegal logging, forest burning, burning fields, excessive use of pesticides, and excessive release of smoke from vehicles
questions.correctAnswer: Illegal logging, forest burning, burning fields, excessive use of pesticides, and excessive release of smoke from vehicles

questions.question: Which of the following is a way to reduce interference with the carbon and oxygen cycles?
In the oxygen cycle, oxygen is continuously taken from the atmosphere by which processes?
What process balances oxygen removal by releasing oxygen back to the air?
